title: echium vulgare subsp. granatense
intro: Echium vulgare subsp. granatense, also known as Viper's bugloss, is a subspecies of the Echium vulgare plant, a flowering plant in the borage family native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. The health benefits of Echium vulgare subsp. granatense include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin conditions like eczema and acne. Echium vulgare subsp. granatense can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various methods of harnessing its medicinal properties.
